 {"id":"bd-285p","title":"State checkpointing for safe restart","description":"## What
Persist minimal state needed to resume captures after restart.

## Why
Avoids duplicate ingestion and minimizes data loss.

## How
- Store last pane cursor offsets and sequence numbers
- Restore on startup before capture loop begins

## Success Criteria
- Restart resumes without duplicate segments
- Checkpoint format is versioned","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:52.864538287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:34.316777237Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-285p","depends_on_id":"bd-1a40","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:52.875026792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-285p","depends_on_id":"bd-k0td","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:34.316736521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29hb","title":"Query linting + suggestions","description":"## What
Implement lint rules for common search mistakes and suggest fixes.

## Why
Users should understand why a query failed and how to fix it.

## How
- Parse query, detect invalid operators, unbalanced quotes, unsupported tokens
- Return structured suggestions for CLI/TUI

## Success Criteria
- Lint output is actionable and deterministic
- No raw snippet leakage in error text","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:34.409228020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:12:30.882402265Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29hb","depends_on_id":"bd-2i2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:09:34.418720473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-29hb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented FTS query linting with actionable suggestions + severity, wired into CLI/robot search error handling, and added unit tests for lint rules.","created_at":"2026-02-04T08:11:52Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-29hb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Work complete; attempted close but blocked due to parent status (bd-2i2f parent-blocked).","created_at":"2026-02-04T08:12:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b2i","title":"E2E: profile switch + bookmarks","description":"## Scenarios
- Create profile, apply, verify ruleset changes
- Add bookmarks and filter panes by alias

## Logging
- Capture CLI output and active ruleset summary

## Success Criteria
- E2E artifacts show deterministic profile switching","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:12.775340169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:41.827405986Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b2i","depends_on_id":"bd-2y8l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:39.756542560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b2i","depends_on_id":"bd-37zz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:12.784746825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b2i","depends_on_id":"bd-xkcj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:41.827377854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2gce","title":"CLI/robot/MCP: annotate + label + triage","description":"## What
Expose annotation and triage operations through CLI and machine APIs.

## Why
Automation and external tools need structured access to annotations.

## How
- CLI: `wa events annotate`, `wa events label`, `wa events triage`
- Robot/MCP endpoints mirror CLI and reuse storage layer
- Ensure redaction and audit logging

## Success Criteria
- Commands update and fetch annotations deterministically
- Robot/MCP schema outputs include notes/labels/state","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:21.709898800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:32.964336320Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gce","depends_on_id":"bd-1yk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:03.145501956Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gce","depends_on_id":"bd-2sua","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:21.723786089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gce","depends_on_id":"wa-5em.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:16:32.964297368Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hnp","title":"Implement Slack/Discord/email/webhook senders","description":"## What
Implement channel-specific senders using the unified notification interface.

## Why
Operators rely on existing collaboration tools; we should reuse existing webhook templates and add email without duplicating logic.

## How
- Slack/Discord: reuse existing webhook template renderers
- Generic webhook: existing path + new interface
- Email: add SMTP sender with redacted summaries

## Success Criteria
- Each sender validates config and handles failures gracefully
- Payloads include redacted summaries and actionable links","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:08.242237143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:49.729857838Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hnp","depends_on_id":"bd-2uyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:51.689912324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hnp","depends_on_id":"bd-ugaj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:08.255555943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hvc","title":"Diagnostics: restart history + backoff status","description":"## What
Expose restart history and backoff status in diagnostics.

## Why
Operators need to understand stability issues quickly.

## How
- Extend health snapshot with restart counts and last crash
- Add CLI output in `wa status`/`wa triage`

## Success Criteria
- Restart history visible without verbose flags
- Output remains redacted","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:01.046792031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:39.854091819Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hvc","depends_on_id":"bd-1a40","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:01.054348419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hvc","depends_on_id":"bd-285p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:39.854066332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hvc","depends_on_id":"bd-k0td","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:37.699079349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -62,7 +62,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-33v2","title":"[EPIC] Audit log streaming + tailing","description":"## Background
Audit logs exist but are hard to consume in real time or integrate with external tooling.

## Goals
- Provide a streaming/tailing interface for audit logs
- Offer JSONL output for external ingestion (SIEM/log shipper)
- Ensure redaction and rate limiting

## Non-Goals
- External log shipping agent

## Considerations
- Streaming must not block writer threads
- Output must remain data-only (no ANSI)

## Success Criteria
- `wa audit tail` streams JSONL reliably
- Unit + e2e tests validate streaming and redaction","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:17:42.405083590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:23.703162816Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33v2","depends_on_id":"wa-5em.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:23.703132049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33v2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:41.540926300Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35v5","title":"E2E: search linting + rebuild","description":"## Scenarios
- Invalid query returns lint suggestions
- Verify/rebuild commands produce stable results

## Logging
- Capture CLI JSON output and timing
- Capture FTS integrity stats

## Success Criteria
- E2E artifacts show lint output and rebuild success","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:13.053985395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:25:42.637187983Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35v5","depends_on_id":"bd-29hb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:29.117691807Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35v5","depends_on_id":"bd-2i2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:13.061097146Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35v5","depends_on_id":"bd-3hoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:32.876776326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-35v5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added E2E scenario search_linting_rebuild covering wa search linting + fts verify/rebuild (scripts/e2e_test.sh).","created_at":"2026-02-04T08:25:29Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-35v5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Work complete; close blocked because bd-29hb and bd-3hoe still in_progress (parent-blocked).","created_at":"2026-02-04T08:25:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35zb","title":"E2E artifact packer: capture logs/outputs on failure","description":"## Goal
Implement reusable helpers that collect detailed logs and artifacts for every E2E script.

## Requirements
- Wrapper to capture stdout/stderr, env snapshot, and JSON outputs.
- Artifact bundling with stable directory layout + manifest.
- Explicit hooks for redaction and size limits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts can opt-in with a single wrapper call.
- Failures always include the full artifact bundle in CI.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:36:26.531001814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:55:16.862716895Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:55:16.862356517Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E artifact packer library with full functionality: capture/redaction/size-limiting/manifest generation. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35zb","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-37f9","title":"Implement saved search storage + CLI commands","description":"## What
Implement persistence and CLI CRUD for saved searches.

## Why
Operators need a stable interface to create, list, run, and delete searches.

## How
- Add storage table + queries (create/list/get/update/delete)
- CLI: `wa search save`, `wa search saved list`, `wa search saved run`, `wa search saved delete`
- Reuse existing search execution functions; no duplicate FTS logic

## Risks
- Migration needed for new table; ensure schema migration handled.

## Success Criteria
- CLI operations round-trip correctly
- Storage layer returns deterministic ordering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:33.707928893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:23.163004813Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:33.721036351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-yt9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:23.162974316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-37f9","title":"Implement saved search storage + CLI commands","description":"## What
Implement persistence and CLI CRUD for saved searches.

## Why
Operators need a stable interface to create, list, run, and delete searches.

## How
- Add storage table + queries (create/list/get/update/delete)
- CLI: `wa search save`, `wa search saved list`, `wa search saved run`, `wa search saved delete`
- Reuse existing search execution functions; no duplicate FTS logic

## Risks
- Migration needed for new table; ensure schema migration handled.

## Success Criteria
- CLI operations round-trip correctly
- Storage layer returns deterministic ordering","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanForge","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:33.707928893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:39:51.529897346Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:33.721036351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-37f9","depends_on_id":"bd-yt9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:23.162974316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-37zz","title":"[EPIC] Ruleset profiles + pane bookmarks","description":"## Background
Operators often want different rule packs and quick access to specific panes without complex filters.

## Goals
- Named ruleset profiles (enable/disable packs by agent/workspace)
- Pane bookmarks/aliases for quick jump and filters
- Integrate with CLI/TUI without duplicating logic

## Non-Goals
- Full workflow automation (covered elsewhere)

## Considerations
- Profiles must be safe to switch while watcher runs
- Bookmarks should not leak sensitive pane titles

## Success Criteria
- Ruleset profile switching is deterministic
- Bookmarked panes are discoverable and filterable
- Unit + e2e tests for profile switching and bookmarks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:14:10.846381056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:14:10.846381056Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-38et","title":"E2E: perf regression smoke with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Validate performance optimizations end-to-end without regressions, with high-fidelity logs.

## Requirements
- Run a representative high-load scenario (many panes / large transcripts) using existing fixtures.
- Capture timing metrics and verbose logs as artifacts (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS query).
- Assert performance budgets are met and output remains correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E perf smoke passes locally and in CI.
- On failure, artifacts include full logs, timing metrics, and scenario parameters.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:32.127057017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:42.346832169Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-396b","title":"[EPIC] Pane priority + capture budgets","description":"## Background
High-volume panes can starve others and drive storage/CPU spikes. Operators need a way to prioritize critical panes.

## Goals
- Per-pane priority/weighting for capture scheduling
- Enforce capture budgets to prevent overload
- Expose diagnostics for throttling decisions

## Non-Goals
- Manual pausing of panes (covered by policy/filters)

## Considerations
- Must preserve deterministic ordering for tests
- Priorities should be configurable at runtime

## Success Criteria
- Capture scheduler honors priorities under load
- Budget limits are observable and adjustable
- Unit + e2e tests cover burst scenarios","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:26.257040963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:04:26.257040963Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
